world war 2: World War II (1939-1945). Global conflict between the Allies (US, UK, USSR, China) and the Axis (Germany, Japan, Italy). Over 70 million casualties. Ended with atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki.
cold war: The Cold War (1947-1991) was geopolitical tension between the US and Soviet Union. Characterized by nuclear arms race, space race, proxy wars, and ideological conflict.
ancient egypt: Ancient Egyptian civilization flourished along the Nile from c. 3100 BC to 332 BC. Known for pyramids, hieroglyphics, and pharaohs.
the renaissance: Cultural rebirth from the 14th to 17th centuries. Marked by advances in art, science, and philosophy. Key figures: da Vinci, Michelangelo, Galileo.
the industrial revolution: Began in Britain around 1760. Transition from manual to machine manufacturing. Steam power, iron production, textile machinery transformed society.
